channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund 2026: UNHCR & ICVA Open Grants for Local NGOs and CSOs Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
Local and national NGOs working in humanitarian settings now have a major opportunity to secure funding for community protection and awareness initiatives through the Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund 2026. Jointly launched by the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) and the International Council of Voluntary Agencies (ICVA), this initiative is designed […]

The post Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund 2026: UNHCR & ICVA Open Grants for Local NGOs and CSOs Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Local and national NGOs working in humanitarian settings now have a major opportunity to secure funding for community protection and awareness initiatives through the Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund 2026. Jointly launched by the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) and the International Council of Voluntary Agencies (ICVA), this initiative is designed to strengthen grassroots efforts aimed at preventing sexual exploitation and abuse (PSEA) in vulnerable communities.

The 2026 Call for Proposals is officially open, providing grants for organizations that are developing community-centered outreach campaigns, awareness materials, communication strategies, and systems-strengthening activities tailored to local realities, languages, and cultures.

This funding opportunity is particularly important for local humanitarian actors, grassroots organizations, and civil society groups that are directly engaged with displaced populations, refugees, conflict-affected communities, and other vulnerable groups in humanitarian crises.

About the Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund

The Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund was established in 2020 as a collaborative initiative between ICVA and UNHCR. Since its launch, the Fund has focused on empowering local organizations to lead awareness and prevention efforts against sexual exploitation and abuse within humanitarian settings.

The Fund recognizes that local organizations are often best positioned to understand community realities, communicate effectively in local languages, and design culturally appropriate interventions that genuinely resonate with affected populations.

The initiative aims to ensure that people receiving humanitarian assistance:

  • Understand their rights
  • Know that humanitarian aid is never conditional on favors or exchanges
  • Have access to safe reporting channels
  • Are aware of available support systems
  • Can participate in community-led protection mechanisms

By supporting local leadership, the Fund contributes to a more accountable, transparent, and community-driven humanitarian response system.

What Does the Fund Support?

The 2026 PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund supports a wide range of locally driven initiatives that improve awareness, communication, and protection systems related to sexual exploitation and abuse prevention.

1. Community-Led Protection Initiatives

The Fund prioritizes projects that place communities at the center of protection efforts. Organizations are encouraged to involve affected populations directly in the design and implementation of outreach strategies.

Examples include:

  • Community awareness campaigns
  • Peer-to-peer education initiatives
  • Grassroots advocacy programs
  • Survivor-centered communication approaches
  • Community dialogue sessions

2. Locally Tailored Communication Materials

Supported projects are expected to create outreach materials adapted to:

  • Local languages
  • Literacy levels
  • Cultural realities
  • Vulnerable population groups
  • Indigenous communication systems

Materials may include:

  • Posters
  • Radio programs
  • Comics and illustrations
  • Community theatre
  • Videos
  • Social media campaigns
  • Audio messaging
  • Educational toolkits

3. Strengthening Local Leadership in PSEA

The Fund is committed to strengthening local ownership of PSEA initiatives and supporting grassroots organizations in becoming leaders within humanitarian protection systems.

This aligns with major international commitments including:

  • The Grand Bargain localization agenda
  • The Global Compact on Refugees
  • Inter-Agency Standing Committee (IASC) localization commitments

4. Open-Source PSEA Materials and Knowledge Sharing

All outreach materials created through funded projects become part of a publicly accessible deliverables database. This allows organizations globally to adapt and reuse effective communication tools while reducing duplication and improving consistency in humanitarian messaging.

Impact of the PSEA Outreach Fund

Since its establishment, the Fund has already demonstrated measurable results across multiple humanitarian contexts worldwide.

Key Achievements

65+ PSEA Outreach Projects Funded

The initiative has already financed more than 65 grassroots protection and awareness projects implemented by local organizations.

Over 2 Million People Reached

Fund-supported projects have collectively reached more than 2 million people with awareness messaging and protection information.

Outreach Materials Produced in 60+ Languages

Projects have successfully developed culturally adapted communication materials in more than 60 languages, significantly improving accessibility and inclusivity.

More Than 450 Outreach Materials Developed

Organizations have produced hundreds of educational and awareness materials that are now available for adaptation and reuse globally.

Independent Evaluation Findings

An independent evaluation supported by the UK Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO) confirmed the strong impact and strategic importance of the Fund.

Major Findings Include:

84% Increase in Community Awareness

Organizations reported a dramatic increase in awareness related to sexual exploitation and abuse prevention across communities reached.

Increased Reporting Mechanisms

Approximately 85% of participating organizations observed moderate or significant increases in reporting of SEA-related incidents after project implementation.

Stronger Local Organizations

78% of organizations stated that implementing a Fund-supported project significantly strengthened their internal PSEA capacity.

Community Participation Matters

68% of respondents indicated that communities were heavily involved in project design and implementation, increasing trust and effectiveness.

Why This Fund Matters in Humanitarian Settings

Sexual exploitation and abuse remain major concerns in humanitarian contexts where vulnerable populations often face heightened risks and power imbalances.

The PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund addresses these challenges by:

  • Supporting localized protection strategies
  • Increasing awareness among vulnerable groups
  • Promoting safe reporting systems
  • Strengthening accountability
  • Encouraging survivor-centered approaches
  • Building trust between aid providers and communities

Importantly, the Fund moves beyond simple awareness campaigns by encouraging projects that address structural issues and power dynamics that contribute to exploitation and abuse.

Eligibility and Who Can Apply

The 2026 Call for Proposals is primarily aimed at local and national NGOs responding to humanitarian crises.

Organizations applying should demonstrate:

  • Community engagement experience
  • Capacity to implement outreach activities
  • Knowledge of local contexts and languages
  • Commitment to protection and safeguarding principles
  • Ability to develop culturally appropriate communication materials

Projects should focus on humanitarian settings and prioritize at-risk populations.

Application Timeline

Applications Opened:  19 May 2026

Application Deadline:  14 June 2026 at 23:59 CET

Late applications may not be considered, so organizations are encouraged to begin preparing proposals early.

Available Resources for Applicants

Applicants can access guidance and application materials in multiple languages, including:

  • English
  • Arabic
  • French

The Fund also provides access to:

  • PSEA materials database
  • Project summaries from previous funding rounds
  • ICVA PSEAH publications
  • Application guidance documents

Benefits of Participating in the Fund

Organizations selected for funding can benefit from:

  1. Financial support for PSEA outreach projects
  2. Increased organizational visibility
  3. Capacity strengthening opportunities
  4. Access to global PSEA resources
  5. Participation in international humanitarian protection networks
  6. Contribution to community-led safeguarding systems
  7. Opportunities for long-term partnership development

How to Apply

Interested organizations should carefully review the official guidance documents and application materials before submitting proposals.

Applicants are advised to:

  1. Read all application guidance thoroughly
  2. Clearly define community needs
  3. Demonstrate local engagement
  4. Explain sustainability plans
  5. Highlight culturally adapted approaches
  6. Include measurable outcomes and indicators

Apply, Click Here

For more information about the proposals, visit the official website

Final Thoughts

The Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund 2026 represents a significant opportunity for grassroots organizations and humanitarian actors to strengthen protection systems and empower vulnerable communities.

By supporting locally led initiatives, UNHCR and ICVA are reinforcing the importance of community ownership, accountability, and culturally grounded communication in preventing sexual exploitation and abuse in humanitarian contexts.

Organizations with innovative, community-centered outreach ideas are strongly encouraged to apply before the June 14, 2026 deadline.

Opportunity Category

Grant

Eligible Applicants

  • Local NGOs
  • National NGOs
  • Civil Society Organizations (CSOs)
  • Humanitarian organizations
  • Community-led organizations
  • Grassroots protection groups

Eligible Regions

Open to organizations operating in humanitarian settings globally.

Required Qualifications

  • Experience in humanitarian or protection-related work
  • Capacity to implement community outreach activities
  • Understanding of safeguarding and PSEA principles
  • Ability to develop localized communication materials
  • Strong community engagement approach

Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

 

The post Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund 2026: UNHCR & ICVA Open Grants for Local NGOs and CSOs Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Barr Foundation Fellowship 2026: $75,000 Leadership Fellowship for Nonprofit Leaders in Massachusetts
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The Barr Foundation has officially reopened applications for the highly anticipated 2026 Barr Fellowship Program, a transformative leadership initiative designed to support nonprofit leaders navigating today’s increasingly demanding social impact landscape. After a strategic review and relaunch, the Barr Fellowship is returning with a renewed vision focused on resilience, renewal, leadership sustainability, collaboration, and relationship-building […]

The post Barr Foundation Fellowship 2026: $75,000 Leadership Fellowship for Nonprofit Leaders in Massachusetts appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Barr Foundation has officially reopened applications for the highly anticipated 2026 Barr Fellowship Program, a transformative leadership initiative designed to support nonprofit leaders navigating today’s increasingly demanding social impact landscape.

After a strategic review and relaunch, the Barr Fellowship is returning with a renewed vision focused on resilience, renewal, leadership sustainability, collaboration, and relationship-building among nonprofit leaders across Massachusetts.

The Fellowship recognizes extraordinary civic and nonprofit leaders while providing substantial financial support, executive coaching, wellness resources, and long-term networking opportunities that strengthen both individuals and organizations.

Applications for the 2026 Barr Fellows cohort are now open.

About the Barr Fellowship

The Barr Fellowship was first launched in 2005 by the Barr Foundation to celebrate and strengthen exceptional leadership across the nonprofit and social impact sectors.

Over the years, the Fellowship has supported more than 80 leaders across seven cohorts, creating a vibrant network of changemakers working in different fields and communities throughout Massachusetts.

Following the challenges brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic, racial justice movements, increasing climate concerns, and growing pressure on nonprofit organizations, the Foundation conducted an extensive review of the Fellowship program.

The result is a refreshed Fellowship experience built specifically to help leaders:

  • Combat burnout and isolation
  • Improve resilience and sustainability
  • Develop collaborative leadership skills
  • Build stronger professional networks
  • Foster healthier organizational cultures
  • Invest in personal well-being and renewal

The 2026 Fellowship reflects a growing recognition that nonprofit leaders need more than funding alone — they also need support systems, trusted relationships, wellness opportunities, and sustainable leadership practices.

What the Barr Fellowship 2026 Offers

Selected Fellows and their organizations will receive a comprehensive package of financial, professional, and leadership support over a 15-month period.

1. $75,000 Organizational Grant

Each selected Fellow’s organization will receive a total grant of $75,000, divided into two major components:

  • $25,000 dedicated to the Fellow’s personal well-being, leadership development, and renewal
  • $50,000 allocated to organizational resilience, staff wellness, and operational sustainability

This funding structure ensures that both the individual leader and their organization benefit from the Fellowship experience.

2. Executive and Organizational Coaching

Participants will receive 50 hours of coaching support, including:

  • Executive coaching for the Fellow
  • Organizational coaching focused on resilience-building and leadership sustainability
  • Strategic guidance to strengthen organizational culture and collaborative leadership practices

The coaching component is designed to help leaders navigate complex challenges while developing healthier and more effective leadership approaches.

3. Cohort Learning and Leadership Development

Fellows will participate in approximately 16 days of scheduled programming over 15 months.

The program includes:

  • Leadership retreats
  • Peer learning opportunities
  • Relationship-building sessions
  • Collaborative leadership development
  • Networking experiences across sectors

The cohort model allows Fellows to connect deeply with leaders from diverse backgrounds and disciplines across Massachusetts.

4. Lifelong Alumni Network

After completing the program, participants become part of the broader Barr Fellows Network.

This alumni network provides ongoing:

  • Collaboration opportunities
  • Peer support
  • Leadership exchange
  • Strategic partnerships
  • Community engagement opportunities

The network aims to strengthen long-term collaboration among social impact leaders across the Commonwealth.

Program Goals of the Barr Fellowship

The Barr Fellowship seeks to empower nonprofit leaders to:

Increase Personal and Professional Resilience

The program prioritizes leader wellness, helping participants cultivate sustainability, clarity, and renewal in demanding leadership roles.

Strengthen Collaborative Leadership Skills

Fellows gain tools and experiences that improve teamwork, partnership-building, and cross-sector collaboration.

Build Healthy Organizational Cultures

The Fellowship encourages organizations to create environments where staff can thrive, contribute meaningfully, and feel supported.

Expand Leadership Networks

Participants build valuable relationships that foster mutual aid, solidarity, innovation, and long-term social impact collaboration.

Why the Fellowship Matters Today

Nonprofit organizations continue to face immense pressure due to:

  • Reduced resources
  • Increased community needs
  • Workforce burnout
  • Economic uncertainty
  • Social justice challenges
  • Climate-related concerns
  • DEI backlash and organizational stress

The Barr Foundation recognizes that leadership in the social sector can often become isolating and unsustainable without intentional support systems.

The Fellowship was redesigned specifically to address these realities by helping leaders prioritize wellness, resilience, connection, and organizational sustainability.

Eligibility Criteria for the Barr Fellowship 2026

To qualify for the Fellowship, both the organization and the nominated leader must meet specific eligibility requirements.

Organizational Eligibility Requirements

Applicant organizations must:

  • Be a nonprofit 501(c)(3) public charity or fiscally sponsored by one
  • Be headquartered in Massachusetts
  • Conduct at least 75% of activities or programming in Massachusetts
  • Have an operating budget between approximately $500,000 and $5 million
  • Demonstrate strong commitment to justice, belonging, and inclusive leadership
  • Maintain adequate staffing and operational capacity during Fellowship participation

Organizations That Are Not Eligible

The following organizations cannot apply:

  • Government agencies
  • Hospitals
  • K-12 schools
  • Higher education institutions
  • Organizations primarily focused on re-granting funds

Individual Eligibility Requirements

The organization’s nominated candidate must:

  • Serve as CEO, Executive Director, or Co-Director
  • Have held their current role for at least 3 years
  • Have 3–10 years of nonprofit executive leadership experience
  • Be a full-time resident of New England
  • Work professionally with significant focus on Massachusetts
  • Demonstrate commitment to justice, belonging, and healthy organizational culture
  • Commit fully to all Fellowship activities and participation requirements

Barr Fellowship 2026 Application Timeline

Applicants should carefully note the following important dates:

Key Dates

  • Application Opens: May 14, 2026
  • Information Session 1: June 11, 2026 (3–4 PM ET)
  • Information Session 2: June 16, 2026 (1–2 PM ET)
  • Application Deadline: June 30, 2026
  • Advancing Candidates Notification: August 2026
  • Candidate Calls: August 2026
  • Finalist Notifications: Early September 2026
  • Finalist Interviews: Late September to Early October 2026
  • Cohort and Award Announcement: November 2026

Deadline

The application deadline for the Barr Fellowship 2026 is June 30, 2026.

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early and attend one of the scheduled information sessions to better understand the Fellowship expectations and selection process.

How to Apply

Click here for more and to apply

Before applying, candidates should carefully review all eligibility requirements and ensure they can fully participate in the Fellowship’s programming and leadership commitments.

The Barr Fellowship represents a unique opportunity for nonprofit leaders to invest in personal renewal, strengthen organizational culture, expand collaborative leadership capacity, and join a powerful network of social impact leaders across Massachusetts.

For leaders committed to sustainable change and community impact, this Fellowship offers both transformational support and long-term professional connection opportunities.

Click here for more global fellowship opportunities

The post Barr Foundation Fellowship 2026: $75,000 Leadership Fellowship for Nonprofit Leaders in Massachusetts appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Democracy Hackathon 2026 in Strasbourg, France | Fully Funded Opportunity to Build Tech Solutions Against Online Hate
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Democracy Hackathon 2026 is now officially open for applications, offering innovators, developers, designers, democracy experts, and human rights advocates an exciting fully funded opportunity to travel to Strasbourg, France, and develop cutting-edge technology solutions to combat online hate speech and strengthen democratic values across Europe. Organized by the Council of Europe under the New […]

The post Democracy Hackathon 2026 in Strasbourg, France | Fully Funded Opportunity to Build Tech Solutions Against Online Hate appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Democracy Hackathon 2026 is now officially open for applications, offering innovators, developers, designers, democracy experts, and human rights advocates an exciting fully funded opportunity to travel to Strasbourg, France, and develop cutting-edge technology solutions to combat online hate speech and strengthen democratic values across Europe.

Organized by the Council of Europe under the New Democratic Pact for Europe and jointly with No Hate Speech Week, the hackathon aims to bring together some of the brightest minds in technology, democracy promotion, AI ethics, and human rights advocacy.

The prestigious event will take place from 17–19 June 2026 at the Palais de l’Europe in Strasbourg, France, under the auspices of the Monaco Presidency of the Committee of Ministers.

Selected teams will receive:

  • An all-expenses-paid trip to Strasbourg
  • Accommodation and travel support
  • Expert mentoring
  • Access to leading democracy and technology experts
  • A chance to compete for a €50,000 Microsoft grant
  • Post-event mentorship and implementation support

The application deadline is 5 June 2026.

About the Democracy Hackathon 2026

The Democracy Hackathon is more than a technology competition. It is a strategic initiative focused on defending democracy in the digital age.

As democratic systems worldwide face growing threats from:

  • Online hate speech
  • Disinformation
  • Coordinated harassment
  • Digital violence
  • Polarization
  • Malicious online behaviour

The Council of Europe is inviting multidisciplinary teams to create innovative, ethical, and human-rights-centered digital solutions capable of strengthening democratic resilience.

The event feeds directly into the Council of Europe’s broader political initiative called the New Democratic Pact for Europe, which seeks to address democratic backsliding and restore public trust in democratic institutions.

Why This Hackathon Matters

Digital platforms have become central to public debate and democratic participation. However, they have also become spaces where:

  • Hate speech spreads rapidly
  • Coordinated manipulation campaigns flourish
  • Women and minority voices face targeted harassment
  • Privacy risks increase
  • Democratic conversations become increasingly toxic

The Democracy Hackathon seeks practical solutions that:

  • Protect freedom of expression
  • Preserve privacy rights
  • Reduce online harm
  • Empower institutions
  • Promote democratic participation

Rather than focusing solely on content moderation, the hackathon encourages teams to rethink how technology can positively transform online communities and democratic engagement.

Key Benefits of Participating

Selected teams will receive exceptional opportunities and support.

1. Fully Funded Participation

The Council of Europe will provide:

  • Full travel coverage
  • Accommodation support
  • Access to the live event in Strasbourg

This removes financial barriers and ensures broad participation.

2. Compete for a €50,000 Microsoft Grant

Winning teams will share a:

  • €50,000 Microsoft grant

The grant is intended to support the further development and implementation of winning ideas and prototypes.

3. Mentorship From Leading Experts

Participants will receive guidance from experts affiliated with:

  • Technical University of Munich
  • University of Strasbourg
  • Democracy Reporting International
  • Institute for Strategic Dialogue

These mentors will support teams both before and after the hackathon.

4. Networking Opportunities

Participants will engage with:

  • Researchers
  • Civic tech experts
  • Human rights advocates
  • Policymakers
  • AI specialists
  • Democracy practitioners

This creates valuable opportunities for future collaborations and career growth.

5. Real-World Impact

The hackathon’s outcomes will directly contribute to:

  • Democratic policy consultations
  • Online safety strategies
  • Human-rights-based digital governance

Participants will help shape future democratic tools and frameworks in Europe.

Theme: “Hack the Hate, Renew Democracy”

The central theme of the hackathon focuses on building democratic resilience through ethical technology.

Participants are expected to create solutions that:

  • Detect harmful online behaviour
  • Protect vulnerable communities
  • Improve institutional responses
  • Encourage healthier digital engagement
  • Balance innovation with privacy and human rights

Core Thematic Goals

Projects should focus on one or more of the following objectives.

1. Data Privacy Protection

Teams are encouraged to:

  • Develop tools that identify hate speech
  • Preserve user privacy
  • Prevent harmful re-identification
  • Balance utility with data protection

2. Combating Hate Speech

Participants may create systems that:

  • Detect coordinated harassment
  • Identify harmful narratives
  • Track cross-platform abuse patterns

3. Preventing Malicious Doxing

Solutions should ensure:

  • Anonymous users remain protected
  • Detection tools are not weaponized against vulnerable individuals

4. Policy and Institutional Support

Projects can help:

  • Electoral commissions
  • Policy actors
  • Public institutions
  • Trusted flaggers

Better detect, classify, and respond to harmful online behaviour.

5. Social Transformation

The hackathon strongly encourages:

  • Intersectional approaches
  • Inclusive design
  • Community empowerment
  • Educational interventions
  • Positive digital engagement models

Democracy Hackathon Challenges

Participants must choose one of four official challenge tracks.

1. Privacy-Preserving Hate Speech Detection Challenge (PrivHSD)

Mentored by researchers from the Technical University of Munich.

Objective:

Build hate speech detection systems that:

  • Maintain strong privacy protections
  • Operate independently of user identity
  • Avoid harmful surveillance outcomes

Expected Outputs:

  • Prototype
  • Demonstrable concept
  • Research note
  • Privacy-preserving architecture

2. Coordinated Inauthentic Behaviour Identification

Mentored by the Institute for Strategic Dialogue.

Objective:

Create tools capable of:

  • Detecting coordinated online campaigns
  • Tracking hate narratives across platforms
  • Identifying gendered harassment campaigns

Potential Deliverables:

  • Monitoring dashboard
  • Narrative clustering systems
  • Visualized analytical tools

3. Reporting and Response Systems for Online Hate

Mentored by Democracy Reporting International.

Objective:

Develop systems that:

  • Transform online reports into structured evidence
  • Support electoral commissions
  • Improve referral and escalation pathways

Expected Outputs:

  • MVPs
  • Wireframes
  • Referral systems
  • Classification frameworks

4. Think Out of the Box Challenge

Mentored by the University of Strasbourg.

Objective:

Create transformative approaches that:

  • Prevent hate speech
  • Contextualize harmful content
  • Promote empathy and dialogue
  • Empower communities

Potential Solutions:

  • Educational tools
  • Social impact platforms
  • Policy frameworks
  • Moderation interfaces
  • Creative interventions

Who Should Apply?

The hackathon is designed for multidisciplinary teams of 3–4 members.

Ideal teams should include:

1. Technologist / Backend Developer

Responsible for:

  • AI systems
  • NLP tools
  • Detection algorithms
  • Technical implementation

2. Democracy or Policy Expert

Responsible for:

  • Human rights alignment
  • Democratic frameworks
  • Governance insights
  • Ethical guidance

3. Designer / UX/UI Innovator

Responsible for:

  • User experience
  • Interface design
  • Accessibility
  • Visualization

Teams may also add a fourth member if needed.

Gender balance within teams is strongly encouraged.

How to Apply

Step 1: Form a Team

Applicants must create a team of:

  • 3–4 members
  • Diverse skill backgrounds

Step 2: Submit a Concept Summary

Teams must prepare a concept summary of no more than two pages.

The proposal should include:

  • Problem statement
  • Proposed solution
  • Human rights considerations
  • Technical overview
  • Expected impact
  • User experience strategy

Step 3: Selection and Warm-Up Session

Selected teams will:

  • Join a virtual preparation session
  • Refine their ideas with mentors
  • Prepare for the live Strasbourg event

Apply: Click Here to learn more and apply.

Important Dates

Application Deadline:

5 June 2026

Event Dates:

17–19 June 2026

Location:

Palais de l’Europe, Strasbourg, France

What Makes This Opportunity Unique?

Unlike traditional hackathons focused only on coding, the Democracy Hackathon combines:

  • Technology
  • Human rights
  • Democracy
  • Ethics
  • Public policy
  • Social innovation

Participants are not just building apps — they are designing tools that could influence the future of democratic governance and digital safety across Europe.

This hackathon offers a rare opportunity to:

  • Collaborate internationally
  • Engage with policymakers
  • Receive expert mentorship
  • Build impactful civic technology
  • Gain visibility in the democracy and human-rights space

For young innovators passionate about democracy, AI ethics, digital rights, and social impact, this is one of the most meaningful technology competitions of 2026.

Discover more competition opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Democracy Hackathon 2026 in Strasbourg, France | Fully Funded Opportunity to Build Tech Solutions Against Online Hate appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ETH Zurich PhD Scholarship 2026 in Agricultural Economics and Climate Policy in Switzerland | Fully Funded 4-Year Doctoral Opportunity
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
ETH Zurich has officially announced a fully funded PhD Scholarship opportunity for students interested in Agricultural Economics, Climate Policy, Sustainability, and Climate Change Mitigation in Swiss agriculture. The doctoral position is hosted within the Agricultural Economics and Policy Group led by Professor Robert Finger and forms part of the major national research initiative known as […]

The post ETH Zurich PhD Scholarship 2026 in Agricultural Economics and Climate Policy in Switzerland | Fully Funded 4-Year Doctoral Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
ETH Zurich has officially announced a fully funded PhD Scholarship opportunity for students interested in Agricultural Economics, Climate Policy, Sustainability, and Climate Change Mitigation in Swiss agriculture.

The doctoral position is hosted within the Agricultural Economics and Policy Group led by Professor Robert Finger and forms part of the major national research initiative known as the ACHIEVE Project: “Pathways to a Net-Zero Target for Hard-to-Abate Swiss Emissions.”

This prestigious four-year doctoral opportunity offers researchers the chance to work on one of the most urgent global challenges: reducing greenhouse gas emissions in agri-food systems while developing practical and evidence-based climate policy solutions.

The position is based in Zurich, Switzerland, and provides:

  • Full funding for four years
  • Competitive ETH Zurich salary
  • Research and training opportunities
  • International collaboration
  • Strong academic mentorship
  • Career development support

The application deadline is 26 June 2026.

About ETH Zurich

ETH Zurich is internationally recognized as one of the world’s leading universities in science, technology, sustainability, and innovation.

The university is known for:

  • Cutting-edge research
  • Academic excellence
  • Global scientific impact
  • Innovation-driven education
  • Interdisciplinary collaboration

ETH Zurich hosts over 30,000 students and researchers from more than 120 countries and consistently ranks among the top universities globally.

The institution is committed to:

  • Sustainability
  • Climate neutrality
  • Equal opportunity
  • Diversity and inclusion
  • Independent and innovative thinking

Its research environment encourages collaboration across academia, industry, government, and civil society to solve major global challenges.

About the ACHIEVE Project

The PhD scholarship is funded under the national research initiative:

“Pathways to a Net-Zero Target for Hard-to-Abate Swiss Emissions” (ACHIEVE)

The ACHIEVE project brings together:

  • 29 member entities
  • 27 collaboration partners
  • Researchers
  • Public sector institutions
  • Industry actors
  • Civil society stakeholders
  • Policymakers

The initiative focuses on developing:

  • Net-zero emission strategies for Switzerland
  • Climate mitigation pathways
  • Evidence-based policy recommendations
  • Sustainable transition frameworks

Within this project, the Agricultural Economics and Policy Group at ETH Zurich leads a work package focused on reducing greenhouse gas emissions within Swiss agri-food systems.

Research Focus of the PhD Position

The doctoral student will conduct research on:

  • Climate change mitigation in agriculture
  • Agricultural economics and policy
  • Behavioural responses to climate policy
  • Sustainable agri-food systems
  • Farmer decision-making processes

The project specifically investigates the:

  • Economic aspects of climate mitigation
  • Policy effectiveness and efficiency
  • Behavioural dimensions of environmental action
  • Impact of climate policies on Swiss agriculture

Key Research Areas

The selected PhD candidate will work on topics such as:

  • Greenhouse gas emission reduction strategies
  • Farmer behaviour and climate adaptation
  • Agricultural policy instruments
  • Climate-related decision-making
  • Sustainability transitions in agriculture
  • Data-driven agricultural policy analysis
  • Economic incentives for environmental action

Research Methodology and Activities

The doctoral project combines:

  • Economic theory
  • Data collection
  • Survey research
  • Behavioural experiments
  • Quantitative analysis
  • Stakeholder engagement

Major Research Activities Include:

1. Conducting Surveys and Choice Experiments

The candidate will:

  • Design and implement farmer surveys
  • Conduct behavioural choice experiments
  • Gather primary data from Swiss farmers

2. Data Analysis

Responsibilities include:

  • Analyzing survey data
  • Working with secondary datasets
  • Conducting economic and statistical analysis

3. Stakeholder Interaction

The researcher will engage with:

  • Farmers
  • Policymakers
  • Agricultural stakeholders
  • Sustainability experts

4. Collaboration with Researchers

The candidate will collaborate with:

  • PhD students
  • Postdoctoral researchers
  • International project teams
  • Interdisciplinary research groups

5. Student Supervision

The doctoral student will also:

  • Supervise bachelor students
  • Support master’s students

PhD Training and Academic Development

As part of the doctoral trajectory, the selected candidate will:

  • Complete 18 ECTS of PhD-level coursework
  • Participate in advanced academic training
  • Engage in interdisciplinary learning opportunities

ETH Zurich provides extensive mentoring and professional development support throughout the program.

Duration and Start Date

Duration:

  • Fully funded for four years

Employment Type:

  • Full-time
  • Fixed-term position

Planned Start Period:

  • Last quarter of 2026 (October–December)

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet the following criteria.

Educational Qualification

Candidates should hold:

  • A Master’s degree in Agricultural Economics, or
  • A closely related discipline

Applicants close to completing their Master’s degree may also apply.

Language Requirements

Because the project involves direct engagement with Swiss farmers and stakeholders, applicants must demonstrate:

  • C1 proficiency in German or French

Strong English communication and academic writing skills are also highly beneficial.

Skills and Competencies

Applicants should possess:

  • Strong analytical skills
  • Quantitative research abilities
  • Academic writing competence
  • Interest in climate policy and sustainability
  • Ability to work in interdisciplinary environments

Benefits of the ETH Zurich PhD Scholarship

ETH Zurich offers one of the most attractive doctoral research environments in Europe.

Key Benefits Include:

1. Fully Funded Position

  • Competitive salary according to ETH Zurich standards
  • Full-time funding for four years

2. Research Excellence

  • Access to world-class research infrastructure
  • Collaboration with leading researchers

3. Personalized Mentorship

  • Strong academic supervision
  • Career guidance and mentoring

4. International and Diverse Environment

  • Multicultural research community
  • Interdisciplinary collaboration opportunities

5. Career Development Support

The program prepares graduates for careers in:

  • Academia
  • Government
  • Industry
  • International organizations
  • Sustainability policy institutions

6. Continuous Training Opportunities

  • PhD-level courses
  • Skills development workshops
  • Academic networking opportunities

ETH Zurich’s Commitment to Diversity and Sustainability

ETH Zurich strongly promotes:

  • Equality of opportunity
  • Inclusive academic culture
  • Respect for diversity
  • Sustainability-focused research

The university encourages applicants from diverse backgrounds and fosters a research environment where all staff and students can thrive.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit the following as a single PDF:

  1. Cover letter
  2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  3. Contact details of two referees
  4. Academic transcripts

Application Deadline

Deadline: 26 June 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply early and carefully prepare strong research-oriented application materials.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should:

  1. Visit the ETH Zurich online application portal
  2. Prepare all required documents
  3. Submit the application online before the deadline

Important:

  • Applications submitted by email or post will not be accepted

Apply HERE

Why This Opportunity Is Important

Climate change mitigation in agriculture is one of the most pressing global policy challenges today. Agriculture plays a critical role in greenhouse gas emissions, biodiversity protection, food security, and sustainability transitions.

This ETH Zurich PhD Scholarship provides researchers with the opportunity to:

  • Contribute to impactful climate research
  • Influence future agricultural policies
  • Work directly with stakeholders
  • Develop practical sustainability solutions
  • Build a globally respected academic career

For students passionate about sustainability, agricultural economics, climate governance, and environmental policy, this doctoral opportunity represents an exceptional pathway into high-impact international research.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post ETH Zurich PhD Scholarship 2026 in Agricultural Economics and Climate Policy in Switzerland | Fully Funded 4-Year Doctoral Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: EU Delegation to China Funded Internship 2026 in Beijing: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Graduates Interested in Diplomacy, International Relations, Communications, and Translation.
item.dc:creator: Hildah W. Nakafu
item.description:
The European Union Delegation to China has announced an exciting funded internship opportunity for young graduates who are passionate about international relations, diplomacy, communications, media, translation, and EU-China cooperation. The internship program will take place in Beijing, China, and is expected to begin in October 2026. This prestigious internship offers selected candidates the chance to […]

The post EU Delegation to China Funded Internship 2026 in Beijing: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Graduates Interested in Diplomacy, International Relations, Communications, and Translation. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The European Union Delegation to China has announced an exciting funded internship opportunity for young graduates who are passionate about international relations, diplomacy, communications, media, translation, and EU-China cooperation. The internship program will take place in Beijing, China, and is expected to begin in October 2026.

This prestigious internship offers selected candidates the chance to work directly within the EU Delegation to China, gaining practical experience inside an international diplomatic mission while contributing to impactful projects and activities. Interns will have the opportunity to engage with political affairs, media and public diplomacy, translation work, and intercultural communication in a highly professional international environment.

The internship will last for up to six months, and selected candidates will receive a monthly grant to help cover living expenses during their stay in Beijing.

For graduates looking to build careers in diplomacy, public policy, international communications, or global affairs, this internship provides invaluable exposure and professional development opportunities.

About the EU Delegation to China Internship 2026

The EU Delegation to China represents the European Union in China and works to strengthen diplomatic, political, economic, and cultural relations between the European Union and China. Through this internship program, the Delegation seeks motivated young graduates who can support its ongoing activities while gaining first-hand experience in international diplomacy.

Interns will work closely with professionals and experts across various departments within the delegation. The experience is designed to provide practical training, mentorship, and exposure to international policy-making processes and diplomatic engagement.

The internship program is especially suitable for graduates with academic backgrounds or professional interests in:

  • International Relations
  • Diplomacy
  • Political Science
  • European Studies
  • Communications
  • Journalism
  • Media and Public Relations
  • Translation and Interpretation
  • Chinese Studies
  • Language and Linguistics

Internship Areas and Responsibilities

Selected interns may contribute to one or more of the following areas:

1. Political Affairs and EU-China Relations

Interns supporting political affairs will assist in monitoring political developments, conducting research, preparing reports, and contributing to diplomatic initiatives related to EU-China relations.

Responsibilities may include:

  • Researching political and international affairs topics
  • Drafting reports and policy briefs
  • Supporting meetings and diplomatic events
  • Monitoring developments related to EU-China cooperation
  • Assisting with communication between departments and stakeholders

2. Press, Public Information, and Social Media

Candidates interested in communications and media will support the Delegation’s public diplomacy and digital communication activities.

Tasks may include:

  • Managing and creating social media content
  • Supporting public information campaigns
  • Drafting press materials and communication content
  • Monitoring media coverage
  • Assisting with event communication and outreach

3. Translation, Interpretation, and Language Support

Interns with strong language skills may assist with translation and interpretation work to support communication between English-speaking and Chinese-speaking stakeholders.

Responsibilities may include:

  • Translating official documents and communication materials
  • Assisting during meetings and events
  • Providing interpretation support
  • Reviewing language accuracy and consistency
  • Supporting multilingual communication initiatives

Financial Benefits

One of the major highlights of this internship is that it is funded. Selected candidates will receive a monthly grant intended to help with living expenses during the internship period in Beijing.

Benefits include:

  • Monthly financial support
  • Hands-on experience in a diplomatic mission
  • International work exposure
  • Professional networking opportunities
  • Practical experience in diplomacy and communications
  • Opportunity to strengthen international career prospects
  • Exposure to EU-China diplomatic relations

Internship Location

  • EU Delegation to China
  • Beijing, China

Beijing provides interns with the opportunity to experience one of the world’s most influential political and cultural capitals while working in an international environment.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants interested in the internship should meet the following requirements:

  • Hold a relevant university degree
  • Have strong English drafting and writing skills
  • Be fluent in Chinese
  • Possess excellent communication skills
  • Demonstrate interest in diplomacy, international affairs, communications, or translation
  • Hold a valid visa and legal authorization to stay in China throughout the internship period

Candidates who demonstrate professionalism, adaptability, intercultural communication abilities, and strong analytical skills may have an added advantage during the selection process.

Why You Should Apply

This internship is a valuable opportunity for young graduates seeking international professional experience. Working within the EU Delegation to China allows interns to develop practical skills, build international networks, and gain direct insight into diplomatic operations.

Some reasons why this internship stands out include:

  1. Exposure to International Diplomacy
    Interns gain practical understanding of how diplomatic missions operate and engage in international cooperation.
  2. Career Development Opportunities
    The internship strengthens professional profiles for careers in diplomacy, communications, international organizations, media, or public policy.
  3. Multicultural Experience
    Participants work in a diverse and international environment while experiencing life in Beijing.
  4. Professional Networking
    Interns interact with diplomats, policy experts, communication professionals, and international stakeholders.
  5. Skill Development
    The experience enhances research, communication, translation, analytical, and public diplomacy skills.

Application Deadline

The application deadline for the EU Delegation to China Funded Internship 2026 is:

12 June 2026 at 17:00 Beijing Time

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early to avoid last-minute delays.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should apply by email using the contact below:

Email: delegation-china-hr@eeas.europa.eu

Official application and information link:

EU Delegation to China Internship Information

Applicants should ensure they prepare all required documents and verify their visa and legal stay eligibility before applying.

Final Thoughts

The EU Delegation to China Funded Internship 2026 presents an exceptional opportunity for ambitious graduates who aspire to build careers in international relations, diplomacy, communications, or translation. Beyond financial support, the internship offers meaningful professional exposure and the chance to contribute to real diplomatic and international cooperation efforts.

Graduates with strong English and Chinese language skills, excellent communication abilities, and a passion for international engagement are strongly encouraged to apply before the deadline.

FOR MORE INFORMATION ABOUT THIS.  

Discover more Internship opportunities click here

The post EU Delegation to China Funded Internship 2026 in Beijing: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Graduates Interested in Diplomacy, International Relations, Communications, and Translation. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of Trieste Opens Applications for 15 Fully Research-Focused PhD Programmes in Italy for 2026/2027
item.dc:creator: Hildah W. Nakafu
item.description:
The University of Trieste has officially launched its highly anticipated Call for Applications for the 42nd Cycle of PhD Programmes for the 2026/2027 academic year. This prestigious doctoral recruitment initiative presents a major opportunity for graduates, researchers, and aspiring academics from around the world to pursue advanced research training in one of Europe’s most research-intensive […]

The post University of Trieste Opens Applications for 15 Fully Research-Focused PhD Programmes in Italy for 2026/2027 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Trieste has officially launched its highly anticipated Call for Applications for the 42nd Cycle of PhD Programmes for the 2026/2027 academic year. This prestigious doctoral recruitment initiative presents a major opportunity for graduates, researchers, and aspiring academics from around the world to pursue advanced research training in one of Europe’s most research-intensive environments.

Located in northeastern Italy, the University of Trieste is internationally recognized for academic excellence, interdisciplinary collaboration, and strong partnerships with scientific institutions across Europe. The latest call includes 15 doctoral programmes spanning science, engineering, medicine, law, humanities, artificial intelligence, environmental studies, and social sciences.

For students and professionals seeking funded PhD opportunities in Europe, this call stands out as one of the most comprehensive and competitive doctoral opportunities available in Italy this year.

Deadline for Applications

The official application deadline is:

25 May 2026 at 12:59 PM (local time)

Applicants are strongly encouraged to complete and submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical difficulties.

Why Study at the University of Trieste?

The University of Trieste continues to attract international researchers because of its strong research culture, global outlook, and strategic location within one of Europe’s leading scientific ecosystems.

Trieste is widely regarded as one of the continent’s most research-oriented cities and hosts numerous internationally respected institutions and innovation hubs, including AREA Science Park and several advanced research centers.

Pursuing a PhD at the University of Trieste offers students the opportunity to engage in cutting-edge research while benefiting from Italy’s rich academic heritage and high quality of life.

Key Benefits of the PhD Programmes

Applicants selected into the doctoral programmes may benefit from:

  1. Access to internationally competitive research environments
  2. Opportunities for fully funded doctoral scholarships
  3. Collaboration with leading researchers and institutions
  4. Strong interdisciplinary learning and research exposure
  5. Potential industry-linked research opportunities
  6. Online interview options for international applicants
  7. Access to advanced laboratories and research infrastructure
  8. European academic networking opportunities
  9. A multicultural and globally connected academic community
  10. Living and studying in one of Italy’s intellectually vibrant cities

List of Available PhD Programmes

The 42nd Cycle of PhD Programmes includes the following doctoral fields:

Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics

  • Applied Data Science and AI
  • Physics
  • Nanotechnology
  • Chemistry
  • Circular Economy
  • Civil-Environmental Engineering and Architecture
  • Industrial and Information Engineering
  • Earth Science, Fluid-Dynamics and Mathematics: Interactions and Methods

Health and Biomedical Sciences

  • Molecular Biomedicine
  • Personalized Medicine and Innovative Therapies
  • Neural and Cognitive Sciences
  • Biodiversity, Evolution and Adaptation

Humanities, Law, and Social Sciences

  • Philosophy, History and Political Studies
  • Advanced Interdisciplinary Legal Studies
  • Transcultural and Transmedia Studies: Literature, Linguistics, Interpreting and Translation

This wide range of disciplines makes the opportunity suitable for candidates from nearly every academic background.

Who Should Apply?

The University of Trieste PhD programmes are ideal for:

  • Graduates interested in pursuing academic research careers
  • Individuals passionate about innovation and scientific discovery
  • Researchers seeking interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Students aiming to study in Europe
  • Professionals looking to deepen expertise in specialized fields
  • Candidates interested in Artificial Intelligence, medicine, law, engineering, humanities, and environmental science

The call is open to both Italian and international applicants.

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for admission into the doctoral programmes, applicants must hold an academic qualification that grants access to doctoral studies.

Eligible qualifications include:

  • An Italian Laurea Magistrale degree
  • An equivalent foreign university degree
  • Any academic qualification recognized for doctoral study access in the country where it was awarded

Applicants should carefully review the requirements for their selected programme, as some doctoral tracks may have additional eligibility conditions.

Selection Process

Admission into the University of Trieste PhD programmes is highly competitive and based on academic merit.

The selection process may include:

1. Academic Evaluation

Applicants’ educational background, transcripts, and qualifications will be reviewed.

2. Research Proposal Assessment

Certain programmes may require candidates to submit a detailed research proposal outlining intended research interests and objectives.

3. Interview

Shortlisted applicants may be invited for interviews conducted either:

  • In person, or
  • Via videoconference for international candidates

Each doctoral programme has specific admission procedures and evaluation criteria outlined in the official programme annexes.

Funding Opportunities Available

One of the major highlights of this doctoral call is the availability of funded positions.

Many selected candidates may receive:

  • Fully funded PhD scholarships
  • Tuition fee waivers
  • Social security coverage
  • Research funding support
  • Opportunities linked to specialized research projects
  • Potential collaborations with industry and innovation sectors

The number and structure of funded positions vary across programmes.

Required Application Documents

Depending on the chosen programme, applicants may need to submit:

  • Academic transcripts
  • Degree certificates
  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Motivation letter
  • Research proposal
  • Reference letters
  • Language proficiency documents

Applicants are advised to carefully consult programme-specific requirements before applying.

Why This Opportunity Matters

The University of Trieste’s 2026/2027 doctoral call represents more than a standard academic opportunity. It offers a pathway into Europe’s growing research ecosystem, providing students with access to international collaborations, high-impact scientific work, and professional advancement opportunities.

For candidates seeking a fully immersive research experience in Italy, the University of Trieste provides a strong combination of academic excellence, interdisciplinary innovation, and global visibility.

The broad range of disciplines also ensures opportunities for students from both STEM and non-STEM backgrounds.

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted online through the official University of Trieste admissions portal.

Official Application Portal

University of Trieste Admissions Portal

Official Deadline

25 May 2026 at 12:59 PM (local time)

For more information about this opportunity click here

Discover more PhD Scholarship opportunities on OFY here

The post University of Trieste Opens Applications for 15 Fully Research-Focused PhD Programmes in Italy for 2026/2027 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Call for Applications: SEAP Fellows 2026 — Fully Remote Human Rights Fellowship for Early-Career Professionals in Asia-Pacific
item.dc:creator: Hildah W. Nakafu
item.description:
The 2026 SEAP Fellowship Programme by Nationality for All (NFA) presents an outstanding opportunity for early-career human rights professionals across the Asia-Pacific region to contribute to one of the region’s most significant research initiatives on statelessness, nationality rights, and legal identity systems. As part of its continued work on the Statelessness Encyclopedia Asia Pacific (SEAP), […]

The post Call for Applications: SEAP Fellows 2026 — Fully Remote Human Rights Fellowship for Early-Career Professionals in Asia-Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The 2026 SEAP Fellowship Programme by Nationality for All (NFA) presents an outstanding opportunity for early-career human rights professionals across the Asia-Pacific region to contribute to one of the region’s most significant research initiatives on statelessness, nationality rights, and legal identity systems.

As part of its continued work on the Statelessness Encyclopedia Asia Pacific (SEAP), NFA is recruiting two full-time fellows for a three-month remote fellowship running from June 15, 2026 to September 11, 2026. The programme is designed to strengthen regional research, advocacy, and knowledge production concerning statelessness and exclusion across Southeast Asia, East Asia, and Central Asia.

Applications close on May 25, 2026 (EOD Bangkok Time).

What is the SEAP Fellowship Programme?

The SEAP Fellowship Programme is a structured research fellowship aimed at supporting the development and verification of the Second Edition of the Statelessness Encyclopedia Asia Pacific (SEAP).

The programme allows selected fellows to work directly with an international human rights organization while gaining hands-on experience in:

  • Human rights research
  • Factchecking methodologies
  • Citation management
  • Legal and policy analysis
  • Website content updating
  • Research documentation
  • Regional advocacy initiatives

The fellowship is entirely remote, making it accessible to qualified candidates throughout the Asia-Pacific region.

About the Statelessness Encyclopedia Asia Pacific (SEAP)

The Statelessness Encyclopedia Asia Pacific (SEAP) is one of the region’s most comprehensive research resources documenting issues related to:

  • Statelessness
  • Nationality laws
  • Citizenship rights
  • Legal identity systems
  • Human rights exclusion
  • Discrimination affecting vulnerable populations

SEAP serves as a major regional knowledge platform that supports researchers, policymakers, advocates, civil society actors, and human rights organizations working to address nationality and identity-related challenges across Asia-Pacific.

Over the years, the project has compiled extensive information on populations affected by statelessness and nationality discrimination throughout the region. The Second Edition of SEAP aims to further strengthen this research through updated factchecking, legal verification, and citation integration.

Fellowship Duration and Structure

The fellowship will run for three months on a full-time basis.

Fellowship Timeline

  • Start Date: June 15, 2026
  • End Date: September 11, 2026
  • Duration: 3 Months
  • Mode: Remote (Work-from-home)

Although the programme is remote, fellows may occasionally participate in in-person meetings if necessary.

Monthly Stipend

Selected fellows will receive a monthly stipend ranging between:

  • AUD 200 – AUD 400 per month

The exact amount will depend on the fellow’s location.

Key Responsibilities of SEAP Fellows

The selected fellows will support the NFA research team in updating and verifying SEAP’s Second Edition.

1. Factchecking and Additional Research

Fellows will:

  • Review existing SEAP country factsheets
  • Conduct supplementary desk research
  • Verify legal, policy, and statistical information
  • Identify outdated or inconsistent information
  • Cross-check regional human rights documentation

2. Website and Report Updates

Responsibilities include:

  • Updating SEAP website content
  • Revising research reports
  • Ensuring consistency across materials
  • Coordinating edits with the NFA team

3. Citation Integration and Verification

Fellows will also:

  • Add missing citations
  • Verify citation accuracy
  • Ensure source integrity
  • Maintain consistency in referencing standards

Detailed Fellowship Work Plan

Month 1: Orientation and Factchecking

During the first month, fellows will receive onboarding and practical training on:

  • Factchecking methodologies
  • Citation systems
  • Website updating procedures
  • Research verification techniques

Fellows will begin reviewing and factchecking assigned regional content during this stage.

Month 2: Website and Report Revisions

The second month focuses on:

  • Updating website materials
  • Revising reports based on verified findings
  • Collaborating with the NFA research team
  • Improving content consistency and accuracy

Month 3: Citation Integration and Final Verification

The final month involves:

  • Integrating citations into SEAP content
  • Conducting final quality checks
  • Verifying accuracy across reports and website entries
  • Finalizing updates for publication

Training and Mentorship

Selected fellows will receive extensive support throughout the programme, including:

  • A detailed factchecking template and research guide
  • Training on citation standards
  • Guidance on website updating systems
  • Ongoing mentorship from the NFA research team
  • Continuous feedback and support

This makes the programme highly beneficial for individuals seeking practical experience in international human rights research and documentation.

Eligibility Criteria for SEAP Fellows 2026

Applicants are expected to demonstrate strong academic, analytical, and organizational abilities.

Required Skills

Candidates should possess:

  • Strong desk research skills
  • Excellent analytical ability
  • Good writing skills
  • Attention to detail
  • Strong organizational skills
  • Effective time-management abilities
  • Ability to synthesize information clearly

Since the fellowship is fully remote, applicants must also be:

  • Self-driven
  • Proactive
  • Comfortable working independently
  • Reliable in meeting deadlines
  • Consistent in remote communication

Academic and Knowledge Background

Applicants should ideally have academic or professional exposure to:

  • Human rights
  • Law
  • Public policy
  • International relations
  • Related disciplines

Knowledge of the following areas is highly desirable:

  • Citizenship rights
  • Statelessness
  • Legal identity systems

However, prior expertise in these topics is not mandatory.

Preferred Experience

Although prior professional experience is not required, applicants with experience in the following areas may have an advantage:

  • Human rights initiatives
  • Research projects
  • Civil society organizations
  • Non-profit organizations
  • Advocacy programmes

Importantly, persons with lived experience of statelessness will receive priority consideration.

Why You Should Apply for the SEAP Fellowship Programme

The SEAP Fellowship offers more than just research experience. It provides fellows with an opportunity to directly contribute to meaningful regional advocacy work while building highly transferable professional skills.

Benefits of the Fellowship

Participants will have the opportunity to:

  1. Contribute to a major regional human rights initiative
  2. Gain expertise in nationality rights and statelessness
  3. Develop professional research and factchecking skills
  4. Learn citation management and documentation systems
  5. Work with an international human rights organization
  6. Build experience in regional advocacy and knowledge production
  7. Strengthen their professional profile in human rights research

For early-career professionals interested in international development, policy research, migration studies, citizenship rights, or legal identity systems, this fellowship provides valuable practical exposure.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit the following materials:

  • Updated CV
  • Cover Letter
  • One original writing sample

Application Deadline

The deadline for submitting applications is:

May 25, 2026 (End of Day Bangkok Time)

Late submissions may not be considered.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should send all required application documents via email to:

info@nationalityforall.org

Application – SEAP Fellows 2026

For more information about the organization and fellowship programme, visit Nationality for All (NFA) Official Website.

The SEAP Fellowship Programme 2026 represents a valuable opportunity for emerging human rights professionals across Asia-Pacific to gain meaningful research experience while contributing to an important regional initiative focused on statelessness, nationality rights, and legal identity systems.

The fully remote structure, practical training, mentorship opportunities, and direct involvement in a major research platform make this fellowship especially attractive for individuals seeking to build careers in human rights, policy research, advocacy, and international development.

For more information about this click here.

Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY here

The post Call for Applications: SEAP Fellows 2026 — Fully Remote Human Rights Fellowship for Early-Career Professionals in Asia-Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: JUST Fellowship 2026 in India: Fully Funded Field-Based Fellowship on Just and Sustainable Transitions
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The Centre for Financial Accountability (CFA), in collaboration with Samata, has officially launched applications for the Just and Sustainable Transitions (JUST) Fellowship 2026. This unique four-month fellowship programme is designed to support field-based research, storytelling, and documentation around ecological, economic, and social transitions taking place across India. The JUST Fellowship 2026 offers selected fellows the […]

The post JUST Fellowship 2026 in India: Fully Funded Field-Based Fellowship on Just and Sustainable Transitions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Centre for Financial Accountability (CFA), in collaboration with Samata, has officially launched applications for the Just and Sustainable Transitions (JUST) Fellowship 2026. This unique four-month fellowship programme is designed to support field-based research, storytelling, and documentation around ecological, economic, and social transitions taking place across India.

The JUST Fellowship 2026 offers selected fellows the opportunity to engage directly with communities experiencing the impacts of mining, industrialization, climate transitions, ecological degradation, and shifting livelihoods. Through grounded research and community engagement, fellows will contribute to shaping conversations around equitable, democratic, and ecologically sustainable futures.

The fellowship particularly focuses on understanding how communities across India are navigating the realities of energy transitions, resource extraction, environmental degradation, labour transitions, governance challenges, and alternative development pathways.

About the JUST Fellowship 2026

The Just and Sustainable Transitions (JUST) Fellowship is a field-based initiative rooted in the idea that “just transition” goes far beyond simply replacing fossil fuels with renewable energy.

The fellowship explores broader structural and societal questions including:

  • Livelihoods and labour transitions
  • Land rights and governance
  • Ecological sustainability
  • Community participation
  • Public finance and welfare systems
  • Resource governance
  • Environmental justice
  • Decentralised economies
  • Community-led alternatives

Across India, communities are facing rapid transformations driven by:

  • Mining projects
  • Industrial expansion
  • Infrastructure development
  • Agricultural transitions
  • Conservation projects
  • Energy transition corridors

While many of these changes bring economic shifts, they also often result in:

  • Displacement
  • Ecological destruction
  • Loss of livelihoods
  • Increasing inequalities
  • Social and environmental conflicts

At the same time, many grassroots organisations, local communities, and collectives are creating innovative alternatives rooted in ecological restoration, democratic governance, and sustainable livelihoods.

The JUST Fellowship aims to document these realities through immersive field-based work and accessible storytelling.

Fellowship Focus Areas

The fellowship encourages applications exploring a wide range of transition-related themes, including but not limited to:

1. Mining and Resource Extraction Regions

Research may focus on communities affected by mining operations, extraction industries, and resource conflicts.

2. Energy Transition Corridors

Applicants may explore how renewable energy projects, energy infrastructure, and decarbonization efforts are impacting local populations.

3. Industrial and Emerging Industrial Zones

Projects may investigate industrial expansion, labour transitions, environmental impacts, and governance structures.

4. Agriculture and Agroecological Transitions

The fellowship supports work related to sustainable agriculture, agroecology, food systems, and rural livelihoods.

5. Ecological Restoration and Conservation Landscapes

Fellows may document community-led conservation efforts, restoration projects, and biodiversity initiatives.

6. Transport, Infrastructure, and Urban Transitions

Research can examine the social and environmental consequences of urbanisation and infrastructure expansion.

7. Post-Extractive Futures and Community Governance

Applicants are encouraged to explore alternative models of development and community-led governance systems.

8. Public Finance and Welfare Systems

Projects may analyse financial mechanisms, public spending, institutional systems, and welfare approaches connected to transition processes.

Fellowship Duration and Benefits

Selected fellows will receive:

Fellowship Duration

  • Four-month fellowship programme
  • Fellowship period: July to October 2026

Financial Support

  • Monthly stipend of Rs. 40,000
  • Travel support of up to Rs. 10,000 (where required)

Mentorship Support

Participants may receive mentorship support based on:

  • Project needs
  • Availability of thematic mentors
  • Area of specialization

The mentorship component is intended to strengthen fellows’ research, documentation, and storytelling capacities.

Who Can Apply?

The JUST Fellowship 2026 is open to Indian citizens with experience or demonstrated interest in:

  • Livelihoods
  • Labour rights
  • Environment
  • Ecology
  • Governance
  • Public policy
  • Media
  • Community work
  • Social justice
  • Development studies

The organisers particularly encourage applications from:

  • Researchers
  • Journalists
  • Independent writers
  • Practitioners
  • Grassroots workers
  • Community organisers

Applicants should ideally have:

  • 2–3 years of relevant experience
  • Interest in field-based research
  • Strong documentation and writing abilities
  • Commitment to ecological and social justice issues

Fellowship Outputs

Selected fellows will be expected to produce:

1. Long-Form Research Article

  • One in-depth article between 6,000–8,000 words

2. Public-Facing Article or Op-Ed

  • One concise article between 800–1,200 words
  • Adapted for public engagement and wider dissemination

The long-form outputs from all fellows will be compiled into a collective publication by the organisers.

Why the JUST Fellowship Matters

The JUST Fellowship stands out because it bridges multiple fields rather than fitting solely into journalism, academia, or policy research.

The programme creates a space where:

  • Field realities meet public discourse
  • Research connects with storytelling
  • Community experiences shape policy conversations
  • Ecological justice becomes central to transition debates

It also contributes to growing conversations around:

  • Climate justice
  • Democratic governance
  • Sustainable development
  • Equitable transitions
  • Community-led resilience
  • Public accountability

Application Deadline

Applications for the JUST Fellowship 2026 close on:

14 June 2026

Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare their applications early.

How to Apply

Applicants can access the fellowship concept notes in:

  • English
  • Hindi

Apply Here 

For more information about this fellowship visit here

Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY on here

The post JUST Fellowship 2026 in India: Fully Funded Field-Based Fellowship on Just and Sustainable Transitions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge 2026: Global Innovation Challenge in Switzerland for Future Entrepreneurs and Problem Solvers
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge 2026 is now open for ambitious innovators, entrepreneurs, students, researchers, and creative thinkers from around the world who are ready to tackle real-world problems in one of Europe’s most immersive innovation experiences. Hosted by the ETH Entrepreneur Club in Switzerland, the InCube Challenge is a unique five-day innovation competition […]

The post ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge 2026: Global Innovation Challenge in Switzerland for Future Entrepreneurs and Problem Solvers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge 2026 is now open for ambitious innovators, entrepreneurs, students, researchers, and creative thinkers from around the world who are ready to tackle real-world problems in one of Europe’s most immersive innovation experiences.

Hosted by the ETH Entrepreneur Club in Switzerland, the InCube Challenge is a unique five-day innovation competition where selected participants live and work together inside a transparent glass cube while collaborating to solve pressing challenges presented by major global companies and institutions.

Taking place from 26–30 October 2026, this highly competitive program combines entrepreneurship, innovation, teamwork, mentorship, rapid prototyping, networking, leadership development, and live pitching into one unforgettable experience.

Unlike traditional hackathons or startup competitions, the InCube Challenge pushes participants beyond their comfort zones by immersing them in a high-intensity environment where creativity, collaboration, and problem-solving happen in real time under public visibility.

Applications for the ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge 2026 are open from 1 May to 31 May 2026.

What is the ETH InCube Challenge?

The InCube Challenge is an immersive innovation competition organized by the ETH Entrepreneur Club, designed to bring together talented individuals from diverse backgrounds to solve practical industry and societal challenges.

Participants are grouped into multidisciplinary teams and placed inside specially designed glass cubes for five days. During this period, teams collaborate intensively to:

  • Analyze complex real-world problems
  • Develop innovative solutions
  • Prototype ideas rapidly
  • Receive mentorship from experts and industry leaders
  • Prepare final pitches for judges and audiences

The challenge culminates in a live finale where teams present their solutions before executives, innovation leaders, entrepreneurs, judges, and hundreds of students.

The program emphasizes:

  • Entrepreneurship
  • Sustainability
  • Innovation
  • Human-centered problem-solving
  • Technology-driven impact
  • Team collaboration
  • Leadership under pressure

Why the InCube Challenge Stands Out

The ETH InCube Challenge has become one of Europe’s most exciting entrepreneurship and innovation experiences because of its unconventional format and strong industry partnerships.

Participants do not simply attend workshops or seminars. Instead, they become fully immersed in a live innovation ecosystem where they must collaborate, think critically, and build practical solutions in real time.

The challenge allows participants to:

  1. Work directly on real-world industry problems
  2. Collaborate with talented peers from around the world
  3. Receive mentorship from experienced professionals
  4. Gain exposure to multinational companies
  5. Build entrepreneurial and leadership skills
  6. Expand professional networks
  7. Develop creative confidence
  8. Experience innovation under pressure
  9. Present solutions to high-level audiences
  10. Explore Switzerland’s innovation ecosystem

The Complete InCube Journey

The InCube experience is structured into several stages designed to prepare participants for the final innovation challenge.

1. PrepCube – Virtual Kickoff

The journey begins with a virtual onboarding phase where participants:

  • Meet fellow innovators
  • Learn about the challenge themes
  • Understand expectations and workflows
  • Begin early collaboration in immersive online environments

2. OutCube – Swiss Alps Retreat

Before entering the glass cube, participants travel to the Swiss Alps for a team-building and preparation retreat.

This stage helps participants:

  • Build team chemistry
  • Strengthen collaboration
  • Prepare mentally for the challenge
  • Develop early concepts and approaches
  • Experience Switzerland’s unique environment

The OutCube phase is scheduled for 20–24 October 2026.

3. InCube – The Main Challenge

The central part of the experience takes place from 26–30 October 2026.

Participants:

  • Live inside the cube
  • Collaborate intensively with teammates
  • Attend mentoring sessions
  • Conduct brainstorming and prototyping
  • Develop innovative solutions
  • Work under tight timelines

The challenge environment is designed to simulate real startup and innovation pressures.

4. Finale – Pitch and Win

At the end of the challenge, teams pitch their solutions before:

  • Industry leaders
  • Corporate executives
  • Innovation experts
  • Entrepreneurs
  • Students
  • Judges
