The final presentations provide teams with visibility, recognition, networking opportunities, and the possibility of winning prizes.

Real Industry Challenges from Global Companies

One of the most impressive aspects of the InCube Challenge is its collaboration with major international corporations and institutions.

Previous challenge sponsors and partners include:

  • UBS
  • ABB
  • Roche
  • IQVIA
  • Siemens
  • AWS
  • Microsoft
  • Huawei
  • Red Bull
  • Swiss Post
  • Allianz
  • SIX
  • ETH Zurich
  • Axpo
  • Thermo Fisher Scientific
  • Hilti
  • PMI Science
  • BKW
  • Swiss Engineering

Participants work on real corporate and societal problems related to:

  • Artificial intelligence
  • Sustainability
  • Healthcare
  • Climate solutions
  • Energy transition
  • Smart buildings
  • Public engagement
  • Green energy
  • Digital innovation
  • Data analytics
  • Patient experience
  • Infrastructure
  • Technology adoption

Examples of Past Innovation Challenges

Thermo Fisher Scientific Challenge

Participants explored how data could help achieve net-zero sustainability goals within clinical trial services.

Siemens x AWS Challenge

Teams developed AI-driven solutions to improve smart building efficiency, sustainability, and occupant comfort.

Roche x IQVIA Challenge

Participants worked on improving patient and doctor experiences for lower-priority healthcare products.

Axpo Challenge

Teams explored ways to accelerate public adoption of sustainable technologies.

ABB Challenge

Participants focused on solving Switzerland’s energy storage and green energy demand challenges.

TransnetBW Challenge

Teams examined how young people could become more actively involved in the energy transition.

Benefits of Joining the ETH InCube Challenge 2026

Participants in the InCube Challenge gain access to numerous personal and professional benefits.

Key Benefits Include:

  • Exposure to global companies and innovation leaders
  • Hands-on experience solving real-world problems
  • Entrepreneurial and innovation training
  • Team collaboration experience
  • Mentorship from experts
  • Networking opportunities
  • Rapid prototyping experience
  • Leadership development
  • International exposure
  • Access to a global innovation community
  • Public speaking and pitching experience
  • Practical experience in design thinking and innovation methodologies

Who Should Apply?

The ETH InCube Challenge welcomes individuals from diverse academic and professional backgrounds who are passionate about innovation and problem-solving.

Ideal applicants include:

  • University students
  • Graduate students
  • Researchers
  • Entrepreneurs
  • Engineers
  • Designers
  • Innovators
  • Startup founders
  • Creatives
  • Scientists
  • Technology enthusiasts
  • Sustainability advocates

The program values:

  • Curiosity
  • Creativity
  • Teamwork
  • Leadership potential
  • Problem-solving ability
  • Adaptability
  • Entrepreneurial thinking

Important Dates for ETH InCube Challenge 2026

Application Timeline

  • Applications Open: 1 May 2026
  • Applications Close: 31 May 2026
  • Group Interviews: June 2026
  • Individual Interviews: June – July 2026
  • Final Decisions: August 2026
  • OutCube Retreat: 20–24 October 2026
  • InCube Challenge: 26–30 October 2026

Why Switzerland is the Perfect Innovation Hub

Switzerland is globally recognized for:

  • Innovation
  • Entrepreneurship
  • Research excellence
  • Sustainability leadership
  • Technology development

Hosting the challenge at ETH Zurich and across Swiss innovation locations gives participants exposure to one of the world’s strongest entrepreneurial ecosystems.

What Participants Can Expect

Participants joining the InCube Challenge can expect:

  • Intense collaboration
  • Real-world impact
  • Fast-paced innovation
  • Exposure to cutting-edge technologies
  • Career development opportunities
  • Global networking
  • Creative experimentation
  • High-energy teamwork
  • Mentorship-driven learning

The experience is designed not only to develop solutions, but also to transform participants into future innovators and leaders.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply through the official ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge application portal during the application period from 1 May to 31 May 2026.

Applicants should prepare for:

  • Group interviews
  • Individual interviews
  • Collaborative assessments
  • Innovation-focused discussions

Strong applications will typically demonstrate:

  • Passion for innovation
  • Team collaboration skills
  • Leadership potential
  • Curiosity and creativity
  • Interest in solving real-world challenges

Apply Here 

Final Thoughts

The ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge 2026 is more than an innovation competition. It is an immersive entrepreneurial experience designed to push participants beyond traditional learning environments and into real-world innovation.

By combining collaboration, mentorship, sustainability, entrepreneurship, and rapid problem-solving, the challenge creates a powerful environment where participants can develop transformative ideas while building meaningful global connections.

For aspiring innovators who want to gain practical experience, work with leading companies, and become part of an international entrepreneurial ecosystem, the InCube Challenge 2026 offers a truly unique opportunity.

For more information about this competition visit here 

To discover more opportunities on OFY, click here

The post ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge 2026: Global Innovation Challenge in Switzerland for Future Entrepreneurs and Problem Solvers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: J-PAL Europe Policy and Research Associate 2026 in France: Fully Detailed Career Opportunity for Early-Career Researchers and Policy Professionals
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab (J-PAL) Europe has officially opened applications for the prestigious Policy and Research Associate position based in France for 2026. This exciting international opportunity is ideal for early-career professionals who are passionate about evidence-based policymaking, development economics, research, impact evaluation, and public policy. Hosted at the Paris School of […]

The post J-PAL Europe Policy and Research Associate 2026 in France: Fully Detailed Career Opportunity for Early-Career Researchers and Policy Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab (J-PAL) Europe has officially opened applications for the prestigious Policy and Research Associate position based in France for 2026. This exciting international opportunity is ideal for early-career professionals who are passionate about evidence-based policymaking, development economics, research, impact evaluation, and public policy.

Hosted at the Paris School of Economics, this position offers candidates a unique chance to work at the intersection of research, policy development, training, and international collaboration while contributing to global poverty reduction efforts through rigorous scientific evidence.

Applicants interested in policy research jobs in Europe, development economics careers, impact evaluation positions, and international public policy opportunities should pay close attention to this highly competitive role.

About J-PAL Europe

The Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab, widely known as J-PAL, is a globally respected research center dedicated to reducing poverty through evidence-informed policymaking. J-PAL works with a network of more than 1,100 affiliated professors at universities worldwide and is recognized internationally for advancing randomized impact evaluations and rigorous policy research.

J-PAL Europe serves as one of J-PAL’s seven regional offices and focuses on generating and utilizing evidence to shape social programs and policies across Europe and beyond.

This particular role is connected to the “Development Methodologies Chair,” established jointly by:

  • The French Development Agency (AFD)
  • University Paris Sciences et Lettres (PSL)

The chair is led by Nobel Prize-winning economists and J-PAL co-founders:

  • Abhijit Banerjee
  • Esther Duflo

The initiative seeks to strengthen research in development economics and improve policymaking through scientific evidence and innovative methodologies.

Position Overview

Position Title

Policy and Research Associate – J-PAL Europe

Organization

J-PAL Europe

Location

France (based at the Paris School of Economics)

Earliest Start Date

1 August 2026

Latest Start Date

1 September 2026

This role provides hands-on professional experience for individuals interested in:

  • Development economics
  • Public policy
  • Humanitarian policy
  • Impact evaluations
  • Research communication
  • Evidence synthesis
  • Policy outreach
  • International development
  • Research capacity building

The selected candidate will support the creation of new research opportunities, strengthen partnerships, coordinate policy outreach, and contribute to training activities.

Why This Opportunity Is Important

This is not just another research assistant position. The J-PAL Europe Policy and Research Associate role provides direct exposure to:

  • International policymakers
  • Leading economists
  • Evidence-based policy initiatives
  • Research dissemination strategies
  • High-level development institutions
  • Policy conferences and workshops
  • Impact evaluation methodologies

The position is particularly valuable for candidates planning to pursue:

  • A PhD in economics or public policy
  • Careers in international organizations
  • Research leadership roles
  • Policy advisory careers
  • Development consulting
  • Humanitarian policy careers

Main Responsibilities of the Role

The selected candidate will contribute across several important areas.

1. Policy Development and Outreach

The associate will support evidence dissemination and policy engagement through:

  • Building relationships with:
    • Governments
    • International organizations
    • NGOs
    • Private-sector institutions
  • Identifying opportunities for rigorous evaluations
  • Preparing policy communication materials such as:
    • Policy briefs
    • Evaluation summaries
    • Presentations
    • Research insights
  • Translating complex academic findings into accessible formats for policymakers
  • Supporting new partnerships that encourage evidence-based policymaking
  • Writing:
    • Blog articles
    • Opinion editorials
    • Public communication materials
  • Coordinating:
    • Conferences
    • Workshops
    • Dissemination events
    • Panels and stakeholder engagements
  • Supporting outreach to:
    • Refugee-led organizations
    • Policy stakeholders
    • Development actors

2. Research Project Development

The associate will also help identify and support new impact evaluations by:

  • Identifying promising research opportunities
  • Engaging implementing partners
  • Exploring evaluation strategies
  • Supporting proposal development
  • Coordinating with J-PAL researchers
  • Assisting fundraising efforts where needed

This aspect of the role gives candidates direct exposure to real-world research implementation and experimental policy evaluation.

3. Training and Capacity Building

The role includes contributions to training programs designed for:

  • Policymakers
  • Project leaders
  • Researchers
  • Users of evidence-based findings

Responsibilities include:

  • Supporting training sessions
  • Developing learning materials
  • Helping communicate evaluation methodologies to diverse audiences

Professional Development Benefits

J-PAL Europe offers extensive opportunities for professional growth.

Selected candidates will gain access to:

Global Research Networks

Candidates will interact with world-leading researchers and development professionals.

Advanced Research Training

Exposure to:

  • Randomized evaluations
  • Causal inference
  • Quantitative research methodologies
  • Evidence synthesis techniques

International Exposure

Potential travel opportunities for:

  • Conferences
  • Workshops
  • Policy engagements
  • Dissemination activities

Career Advancement

The role is especially beneficial for individuals preparing for:

  • PhD programs
  • Research leadership
  • International development careers
  • Public policy careers

Eligibility Requirements

Candidates must meet several academic and professional requirements.

Educational Qualifications

Applicants should hold a Master’s degree in:

  • Economics
  • Public policy
  • Or a closely related field

Coursework should ideally include:

  • Microeconomics
  • Econometrics
  • Causal inference

Candidates from other disciplines may still qualify if they demonstrate:

  • Strong quantitative research interest
  • Knowledge of randomized evaluations
  • Evidence-based policy engagement

Required Experience

Applicants should have:

  • At least one year of relevant professional experience

Relevant experience may include:

  • Research assistantships
  • Research implementation
  • Policy research
  • Impact evaluations
  • Research capacity-building projects

Preferred Technical Skills

Strong preference will be given to candidates with experience in:

  • Randomized evaluations
  • Impact evaluation methods
  • Quantitative research
  • Evidence synthesis
  • Policy communication

Communication Skills

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • Excellent English writing skills
  • Strong oral communication abilities
  • Ability to communicate with diverse stakeholders

Language Requirements

Required

  • Fluency in English

Preferred

  • French language skills
  • Other European languages

Additional Competencies

Ideal applicants should possess:

  • Excellent organizational skills
  • Strong time management
  • Attention to detail
  • Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously
  • Cultural sensitivity
  • Multicultural teamwork experience
  • International collaboration skills

Interest or experience in the humanitarian sector is considered an added advantage.

Travel Requirements

Candidates should be willing and able to travel internationally for project-related activities and events.

Application Process

Interested applicants must complete the J-PAL/IPA common application.

Applicants should:

  1. Register on the J-PAL application system
  2. Select the “Policy” job category
  3. Choose:
    “Research and Policy Associate – J-PAL Europe”

Apply Here 

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit:

  • CV/Resume
  • Cover letter
  • Reference contacts

The cover letter should explain:

  • Relevant experience
  • Qualifications
  • Motivation for the role
  • Authorization to work in the European Union

Incomplete applications will not be considered.

Important Deadline

Application Deadline:

31 May 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible.

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for interviews.

Why Candidates Should Apply Early

Competitive international research opportunities often receive large application volumes. Applying early may help candidates avoid technical delays and demonstrate strong interest in the position.

Career Opportunities After This Role

This opportunity can open doors to careers in:

  • International development
  • Academic research
  • Public policy consulting
  • Humanitarian organizations
  • Government advisory work
  • International NGOs
  • Multilateral institutions
  • Economic policy research

Many previous J-PAL staff members have gone on to:

  • Top PhD programs
  • International organizations
  • Research institutes
  • Global policy institutions

Final Thoughts

The J-PAL Europe Policy and Research Associate position in France represents one of the strongest international opportunities available for young professionals interested in research-driven policymaking and development economics.

Through direct collaboration with globally recognized researchers and policymakers, candidates will gain exceptional exposure to evidence-based policy work and international development research.

For aspiring economists, public policy professionals, researchers, and development practitioners, this role offers both meaningful impact and long-term career growth.

For more information on this job visit   here

Discover more job opportunities on OFY on here

The post J-PAL Europe Policy and Research Associate 2026 in France: Fully Detailed Career Opportunity for Early-Career Researchers and Policy Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
